title: Buttondown vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Buttondown and Omnisend email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which fits your needs.
intro: Buttondown and Omnisend are two distinct email marketing platforms serving different audiences. Buttondown caters to developers and newsletter writers who value simplicity and markdown support, while Omnisend specializes in ecommerce automation with integrated SMS and push notifications. Choosing between them depends on your business type and technical preferences.
choose_a_if: You're a developer or technical writer who prefers writing newsletters in markdown
You need API access and custom integration capabilities for your email system
You want the most affordable email marketing solution with minimal features
choose_b_if: You operate an ecommerce business and need email combined with SMS marketing
You want pre-built automation workflows and advanced segmentation out of the box
You need omnichannel marketing tools (email, SMS, push) in a single platform

faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your use case. Buttondown excels for independent newsletter writers and developers who value simplicity and markdown support. Omnisend is superior for ecommerce businesses needing integrated SMS, push notifications, and advanced automation workflows.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or Omnisend?
faq.a: Buttondown is more affordable, starting at $9/month compared to Omnisend's $16/month. Additionally, Buttondown's free tier supports 100 subscribers, while Omnisend supports 250 subscribers at no cost, making Omnisend better for bootstrapping larger audiences.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import/export functionality, allowing you to migrate your subscriber lists and email content. However, consider that Buttondown lacks SMS and push notification features, so you'd lose those capabilities in the transition. Plan accordingly before switching.
